title: Absolute & Relative Location: FINAL
description:
In this project, you will use latitude and longitude with Google Maps to identify a country. Then you'll review the questions in the Google Doc template before updating it. You'll learn how latitude and longitude coordinates pinpoint a country’s absolute location and how its neighbors and landmarks illustrate relative location. For each of ten coordinate pairs, you will:

  1. Find the country where the lines intersect and enter its name.
  2. List the countries or bodies of water in a specific direction (north, south, east, west, etc.).
  3. Identify and describe a famous physical or cultural place in the region within the target country, using at least two specific, verifiable details.
  4. Provide map evidence by pasting a Google Maps URL or inserting a map screenshot, as directed in each table.


All of your answers should reflect the Five Themes of Geography. Do not alter any of the coordinate or question rows in the template.

sections.title: Absolute & Relative Locations
sections.description:
You’ll start by plotting each site’s absolute location using its latitude and longitude coordinates. Next, you’ll describe its relative location by naming which countries or bodies of water lie to the north, south, east, west, and in the diagonal (NE, NW, SE, SW) directions. Then you’ll pick one physical feature (for example, a mountain range or a wetland ecosystem) or one cultural landmark (like a historic monument or major city) in that area, and support each choice with at least two clear, verifiable facts. Finally, you’ll embed a Google Maps URL or screenshot to prove your location work and sharpen your GIS and spatial-reasoning skills.


For each task, you must do the following.

  1. Enter the correct country name.
  2. List neighboring countries or bodies of water in the direction specified.
  3. Describe a famous place in the region of the country, with two clear facts.
  4. Add the required map link or screenshot for that country.

objectives.title: Table#01
objectives.description: Table#01: Locate the Latitude and Longitude coordinates in Row 1 to find the absolute location in Google Maps. Next, enter the country name in Row 2, then answer the questions in Rows 3, 5, and 7 in the blank row directly below the question.

resources.transcript_summary: This video is a tutorial on how to complete a series of tasks using a table system. The tasks involve using Google Maps to find specific coordinates, identifying the country at those coordinates, and noting down any countries or bodies of water to the south. The tutorial also requires taking screenshots and inserting them into the table, as well as inserting URLs when necessary. The video emphasizes the importance of accuracy and thoroughness in completing the tasks, as the teacher will be reviewing the work, not just for completion, but for correct and relevant information. The tutorial also explains how completed tasks move to a different section of the table, which helps track progress.

sections.title: Physical & Cultural Place: Teacher Graded
sections.description:
Your teacher will evaluate the Physical/Human place descriptions you wrote in Row 6 and the image/URL you input into Row 8 of each table.

  1. Matches the country specified in Row 2.
  2. Directly answers the question in Row 5.
  3. Includes at least two specific, verifiable facts about the place.
  4. Description is well written, with no spelling or grammar errors.
  5. Image or URL matches the country in Row 2.


These are Teacher-Graded tasks, so you will not receive scoring and feedback until after you submit your work to your teacher.

objectives.title: T#1 Teacher Graded
objectives.description:
Table#01: Ensure that the place you describe is located in the country listed in Row 2 and directly answers the question in Row 5. Your description should include at least two specific, verifiable facts. Finally, proofread your response for spelling, grammar, and writing errors.


This is a Teacher-Graded Task. You will not receive scoring and feedback until after you submit your work to your teacher.

data.user_prompt:
{{country}} = United States of America
{{target-table}} = {{Table#01}} 

Parse the JSON from the Google Docs API. Find all `table` objects within the `body.content` array. Then, isolate the table where the text in the first row, second column matches the string {{target-table}}. Only consider the data in the isolated table when you use the ‘Grading Criteria’ to score the student_submission.

‘Grading Criteria’ 
1) Read the country in Row 2 exactly as written (case-insensitive). Read the place named in Row 6. Using real-world geographic knowledge, ask yourself: “Is [place] inside [country]?”. Treat synonyms or official variants (e.g. “United States” vs “United States of America”) as the same country. If no, return a criterion score of 0. If yes, return a criterion score of 5.
2) Read the region in Row 5 and store it as {{region}}. Using world geography, decide whether the place from Row 6 lies within the region of the country from Row 2. Regions are general cardinal directions or named areas (e.g. “East Coast”). Use common definitions: the US East Coast is any coastal area on the Atlantic Ocean. If yes, return a criterion score of 5. If no, return a criterion score of 0 to 4 depending on proximity. 
3) Does Row 6 include at least two distinct, verifiable facts about the place? If no, return a criterion score of 0. If yes, return a criterion score of 5.
4) Evaluate only spelling, grammar, and clarity in Row 6. Ignore factual accuracy.

data.user_prompt:
{{country}} = India
{{target-table}} = {{Table#10}}

Parse the JSON from the Google Docs API. Find all `table` objects within the `body.content` array. Then, isolate the table where the text in the first row, second column matches the string {{target-table}}. Only consider the data in table {{target-table}} when you use the ‘Grading Criteria’ to score the student_submission.

‘Grading Criteria’
1) Read the country in Row 2 exactly as written (case-insensitive), remember it as {{country2}}. Read Row 6, locate the name of the famous place, then remember it as {{famous-place}}. Using real-world geographic knowledge, ask yourself: “Is {{famous-place}} inside {{country2}}?”. Treat synonyms or official variants (e.g. “United States” vs “USA” vs "United States of America") as the same country. If your answer is no, return a criterion score of 0. If your answer is yes, return a criterion score of 5.

2) Read the instruction in Row 5, then locate the regional requirement and store it as {{region}}. Using world geography, decide whether {{famous-place}} lies within the {{region}} region of {{country2}}. Regions are general cardinal directions or named areas (e.g. “East Coast”, "central", "Northern"). Use common definitions: the US East Coast is any coastal area on the Atlantic Ocean within the United States. If yes, return a criterion score of 5. If no, return a criterion score of 0 to 4 depending on proximity. 0 is far away, 4 is near but not within the {{region}} region of {{country2}}.

3) Does Row 6 include at least two distinct, verifiable facts about the place? If no, return a criterion score of 0. If yes, return a criterion score of 5.

4) Evaluate only spelling, grammar, and clarity in Row 6. Ignore factual accuracy.
